Now, another thing you have to consider are your assets and your liabilities. Your assets are all
the things that have value. Anything that brings you money or is worth money is an asset. It's a
good thing. Liabilities are anything that brings you money or is worth money. It's a good thing.
It costs you money or might cost you money in the future. So, anything that has a risk of costing
you money is a liability. So, any loan, any payments you have to make, any interest you have
to pay, these are all liabilities. So, let's say, for example, you bought a car. The car itself is
worth something. So, the car itself is an asset. But you bought the car with financing. It means
you're paying back the dealership. So, the car itself is worth something. So, the car itself is
and you're paying interest. That interest is a liability. And all the things, all the money that
you still owe are liabilities. So, risk management is a very important part of financial planning or
personal finances. Okay? So, you have to keep these things in mind.
